extern type Number;
extern type Bool;
cap Sub { fn sub(a: Self, b: Self): Self }
extern fn js_sub(a: Number, b: Number): Number;
extern fn js_is_zero(n: Number): Bool;
impl Number: Sub { fn sub(a: Self, b: Self): Self = resume(js_sub(a, b)) }

fn count_down(n: Number): Number = if js_is_zero(n) { 0 } else { count_down(Sub.sub(n, 1)) }
fn main(): Number = count_down(5)

===EXPECT===
# Self-recursion: `count_down` references itself inside its body. It is
# classified dep-free (its only cap Perform has a default impl), cloned as
# `count_down__lto`, and the `Sub.sub` call is inlined to a direct
# `js_sub(a, b)` call.
#
# The clone also redirects self-recursive calls to itself, so the loop stays
# in the non-CPS clone and the original CPS `count_down` becomes unreachable
# (DCE'd).
#
# Key invariants:
#   - `count_down__lto` exists with `js_sub(a, b)` directly.
#   - self-recursion inside the clone calls the clone (not the CPS original).
#   - `__main_cps` calls `count_down__lto(...)` directly (no CPS threading).
#   - original CPS `count_down` is DCE'd.
count_down__lto
js_sub(a, b)
return count_down__lto
!function count_down(__caps, n, __k)
!return count_down(__caps,
!count_down__lto(__caps, ((a) =>
